un.caen.model.items

Class Item

  • java.lang.Object
    • un.caen.model.items.Item
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    Energy, Player, Trace, Wall, Weapon

    public abstract class Item
extends java.lang.Object
    Represents an abstract item in the game. This class serves as a base for all specific item types (e.g., bombs, mines, walls). Each item has an ID, visibility status, and a position on the grid.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      protected int id 
      static int ItemCount 
      protected Position position 
      protected java.lang.Boolean visible 

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description

      Constructs an Item and increments the global item count.

      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      int getId()

      Position getNextPosition(Direction direction)
      Gets the next position of the item based on the specified direction.
      Position getPosition()
      Gets the current position of the item.
      java.util.List<Position> getPositionsAround()
      Gets a list of positions around the item based on all possible directions.
      java.lang.Boolean isVisible()
      Checks if the item is visible.
      void setId(int id)
      Sets the ID of the item.
      void setPosition(Position position)
      Sets the position of the item.
      void setVisible(boolean visible)
      Sets the visibility of the item.

    • Method Detail

      • getId

        public int getId()
        Gets the ID of the item.
        Returns:
        the ID of the item

      • setId

        public void setId(int id)
        Sets the ID of the item.
        Parameters:
        id - the new ID of the item

      • isVisible

        public java.lang.Boolean isVisible()
        Checks if the item is visible.
        Returns:
        true if the item is visible, false otherwise

      • setVisible

        public void setVisible(boolean visible)
        Sets the visibility of the item.
        Parameters:
        visible - true to make the item visible, false to hide it

      • getPosition

        public Position getPosition()
        Gets the current position of the item.
        Returns:
        the Position of the item

      • setPosition

        public void setPosition(Position position)
        Sets the position of the item.
        Parameters:
        position - the new Position of the item

      • getNextPosition

        public Position getNextPosition(Direction direction)
        Gets the next position of the item based on the specified direction.
        Parameters:
        direction - the Direction to move
        Returns:
        the Position of the item in the specified direction

      • getPositionsAround

        public java.util.List<Position> getPositionsAround()
        Gets a list of positions around the item based on all possible directions.
        Returns:
        a List of Positions around the item
